risk_rationale: Adds a new read-path (prefix scan) that leverages an existing invariant (NextLeaf sibling chain + tree-wide sorted key order) established and exercised by 1.2.1-1.2.4, but does not modify any existing insert/delete/lookup internals. Elevated from low to medium because: (a) this is the module's first consumer of NextLeaf for anything other than local leaf-split wiring, so any latent NextLeaf-wiring bug from 1.2.3/1.2.4 (including edge cases around the 1.2.4 tombstone-leaf gap) would first become externally observable here; (b) this run also surfaced and had to resolve a scope-correction (the issue's actual 1.2.5 is prefix-scan-only; persist/reload is a separate, not-yet-started 1.2.6), which if missed would have produced an out-of-scope commit spanning two checklist items.
downstream_consumers_not_yet_wired: agents/ingestion (per docs/LLD/btree.md: prefix-scan candidate-shortlisting consumer, not yet implemented)
known_gaps_confirmed_out_of_scope_for_th: No persisted 'current root node ID' across process restarts (belongs to 1.2.6, not this subtask; PrefixScan takes rootNodeID as an explicit caller-supplied parameter, exactly like Lookup and Insert already do)

1.2.4's unreclaimed tombstoned-leaf / abandoned-merged-node-ID gap (verified not to affect scan correctness, since PrefixScan only ever reaches nodes via root-to-leaf descent or NextLeaf, both of which only point at live reachable nodes; reclamation itself remains a distinct, separately-tracked gap)

regression_surface.existing_tests_affected: none (new file only; no existing function signatures changed)
